codeReview.summary: Pass. The diff cleanly retires the admin runtime-policy writer, keeps the company-scoped endpoint as the only public writer, and adds/updates coverage for canonical success, fail-closed validation, foreign/unknown target denial, privileged-path recognition, and OpenAPI contract removal.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared auth-hardening guidance is stale after this feature: it still says company-scoped runtime-policy updates participate in the same policy-control tracking as `/api/v1/admin/tenant-runtime/policy`, even though this packet retires that admin writer and removes its privileged-path handling.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/auth-hardening.md:39 still references `/api/v1/admin/tenant-runtime/policy`, while .factory/library/frontend-handoff.md:236 records that the admin writer was retired and openapi.json no longer contains that path.

summary: Reviewed commit ca51a973 and the worker transcript/handoff for lane01-canonicalize-company-runtime-writer. The implementation satisfies the feature scope and contract expectations; I found no code-level defects, only a stale shared-library note in `.factory/library/auth-hardening.md`.
